files.notes: This file contains two complete articles from the August 1932 issue. The first article 'Literature in the Home' by Elsie C. Carroll runs from the beginning through page 452, followed by 'By Grace Ye Are Saved' by Nephi Jensen which appears to continue beyond the end of this file fragment. Both articles are substantive pieces that match entries in the table of contents.

files.notes: This file contains two distinct sections: 'Notes to the Field' which includes contest announcements and administrative information, and 'Notes from the Field' which contains reports from various stakes and branches. The split occurs at the clear transition between these two different types of content.

splits.title: Lesson Department
splits.start_boundary: Lesson Department Theology and Testimony DOCTRINE AND

files.notes: This is a single comprehensive lesson department containing multiple sections: Theology and Testimony (Doctrine and Covenants), Teachers' Topic (The Beatitudes), Literature (The Delight of Great Books), and Social Service (Child Health and Protection). All sections are part of one unified lesson department guide, not separate articles.
